name: EBV_BamHI_W_IR1_repeat
description: EBV major internal repeat IR1 (BamHI-W repeat): tandem array of 6-12 copies of a 3.072 kb unit located between BamHI-C and BamHI-Y, encompassing the W1/W2 exons that contribute to EBNA-LP and the W promoter (Wp) that drives initial EBNA expression after primary infection. Copy number varies between isolates and is a marker for clonality tracking — single-cell-of-origin clonal expansions retain identical W-repeat counts while polyclonal infections show heterogeneity. The repeat unit contains a BamHI restriction site that defines the BamHI-W fragment of the historical EBV restriction map.
expected_copy_count: 6-12 tandem 3.072 kb units; per-genome ~7 in B95-8
